what_it_does: Defines a user systemd service that launches the Company speech-to-text 'ear' running NVIDIA Parakeet-TDT 0.6b v3 via NeMo on GPU, listening on TCP port 2031, with failure restart and a 10-minute startup timeout.
observations: User-level unit targeting default.target with %h home-directory expansion
ExecStart runs a dedicated Python venv at ~/.voice-venvs/parakeet/bin/python and passes port 2031 as a CLI argument to /home/tim/company/voice/ears/parakeet.py
Optional EnvironmentFile at ~/company/voice/ops/voice.env using the '-' prefix
Comments state a ~3.05 GB VRAM resident footprint and warn not to enable all three STT ears simultaneously on a 16 GB card
Restart=on-failure with a 10-second delay and TimeoutStartSec=600
standouts: VRAM footprint and mutual-exclusion policy are documented directly in the unit comments
Port is passed as a runtime argument rather than configured in an environment file
Uses a model-specific Python virtual environment
concerns: Hardcoded absolute path /home/tim/company/voice/ears/parakeet.py is mixed with %h-relative expansions elsewhere
No explicit dependency or condition for GPU/CUDA readiness
The 'enable only one' policy is only a comment and is not enforced by systemd conflicts or conditions
Model version and port number live only in comments/arguments, not in dedicated configuration keys
notes: This is the systemd launch wrapper for the Parakeet STT worker, carrying both runtime instructions and a manual GPU VRAM arbitration warning.
questions: How does parakeet.py consume the port argument and bind the service?
What mechanism selects this ear as the boot-default versus the other two STT ears?
What variables are expected inside voice.env?
How is GPU availability verified before startup?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it is a systemd unit that starts the Parakeet STT ear on port 2031.
apparent_themes: GPU resource arbitration on a 16 GB card
Operational warnings embedded in unit files
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to make a single GPU STT worker the live default while documenting why the other two must remain on-demand, so operators do not accidentally exhaust VRAM.
novelty: Embedding a VRAM arbitration decision and a 'pick one of three' policy rationale inside a systemd unit file comment.
contribution: Provides the operational launch definition for the Parakeet STT worker, including its port, dedicated venv, restart behavior, startup timeout, and the documented constraint that it must not run alongside the other STT ears.
summary_for_embedding: A user systemd service file that starts the NVIDIA Parakeet-TDT 0.6b v3 NeMo STT worker on GPU port 2031 with restart-on-failure and a VRAM mutual-exclusion note.
intention_for_embedding: To make the Parakeet STT ear the boot-default GPU speech-recognition worker while warning operators to avoid enabling multiple STT ears concurrently.
